entities.name: orforglipron
entities.context: Data are needed regarding the efficacy and safety of the nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist orforglipron as a once-daily oral therapy for weight reduction in adults with obesity.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: Data are needed regarding the efficacy and safety of the nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist orforglipron as a once-daily oral therapy for weight reduction in adults with obesity.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor agonism
entities.context: Daily oral orforglipron, a nonpeptide GLP-1 receptor agonist, was associated with weight reduction.

entities.name: Obesity
entities.context: Obesity is a major risk factor for many leading causes of illness and death worldwide. Data are needed regarding the efficacy and safety of the nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist orforglipron as a once-daily oral therapy for weight reduction in adults with obesity.

entities.name: Diabetes Mellitus, Type 2
entities.context: we enrolled adults with obesity, or with overweight plus at least one weight-related coexisting condition, and without diabetes.

entities.name: GZGI

attributes.primary_endpoint: percentage change from baseline in body weight at week 26
attributes.secondary_endpoint: percentage change from baseline in body weight at week 36
attributes.patient_population: adults with obesity, or overweight plus at least one weight-related coexisting condition, without diabetes

entities.context: In this phase 2, randomized, double-blind trial, we enrolled adults with obesity, or with overweight plus at least one weight-related coexisting condition, and without diabetes. Participants were randomly assigned to receive orforglipron at one of four doses (12, 24, 36, or 45 mg) or placebo once daily for 36 weeks.

entities.name: Gastrointestinal Events

attributes.management: discontinuation in 10 to 17% of participants across dose cohorts

entities.context: The most common adverse events reported with orforglipron were gastrointestinal events, which were mild to moderate, occurred primarily during dose escalation, and led to discontinuation of orforglipron in 10 to 17% of participants across dose cohorts.

entities.context: Title: Daily Oral GLP-1 Receptor Agonist Orforglipron for Adults with Obesity. Authors: Wharton Sean, Blevins Thomas, Connery Lisa, Rosenstock Julio, Raha Sohini. Journal: The New England journal of medicine (2023). PMID: 37351564

entities.name: body weight reduction

entities.name: cardiometabolic risk factors
entities.context: The use of orforglipron led to improvement in all prespecified weight-related and cardiometabolic measures.

relations.evidence: Data are needed regarding the efficacy and safety of the nonpeptide glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist orforglipron as a once-daily oral therapy for weight reduction in adults with obesity.
Daily oral orforglipron, a nonpeptide GLP-1 receptor agonist, was associated with weight reduction.

Daily oral orforglipron, a nonpeptide GLP-1 receptor agonist, was associated with weight reduction. Adverse events reported with orforglipron were similar to those with injectable GLP-1 receptor agonists.
Participants were randomly assigned to receive orforglipron at one of four doses (12, 24, 36, or 45 mg) or placebo once daily for 36 weeks.

The most common adverse events reported with orforglipron were gastrointestinal events, which were mild to moderate, occurred primarily during dose escalation, and led to discontinuation of orforglipron in 10 to 17% of participants across dose cohorts.
(Funded by Eli Lilly; GZGI ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT05051579.)
(Funded by Eli Lilly; GZGI ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT05051579.)

Title: Daily Oral GLP-1 Receptor Agonist Orforglipron for Adults with Obesity. Authors: Wharton Sean, Blevins Thomas, Connery Lisa, Rosenstock Julio, Raha Sohini. Journal: The New England journal of medicine (2023). PMID: 37351564
The percentage change from baseline in body weight was assessed at week 26 (primary end point) and at week 36 (secondary end point).
The use of orforglipron led to improvement in all prespecified weight-related and cardiometabolic measures.
